title: 破镜谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\137-破镜谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Jason Radcliffe explains to Chief Inspector Craddock and Detective Dumot why he remained silent regarding his suspicion that his wife, Marina, was the intended target of a poisoning attempt. Radcliffe admits he believes someone intended to administer a fatal dose of poison to Marina in a public setting, a method that would make prosecution difficult due to the presence of many strangers. He chose to withhold this information to protect Marina’s fragile mental state. He describes her as an actress who seeks constant security but has faced repeated tragedies, including the birth of a son with intellectual disabilities eleven years prior, which resulted in her hospitalization in a sanatorium. Radcliffe notes that Marina had recently regained a sense of calm and optimism, and he feared that informing her of the threat would cause her to collapse again.

When questioned about suspects, Radcliffe refuses to name anyone, stating that while the evidence of an attempted murder is factual, he finds it unbelievable that someone would hate Marina so much. He recounts the events at his home, where he poured two glasses of gin from a pitcher—one for Marina and one for Mrs. Bardok. During the event, while guests including the mayor and movie company representatives were milling around, the drinks were left on a table. Radcliffe believes the poison was added to Marina's glass during this time. The attempt failed when Mrs. Bardok accidentally dropped her glass, and Marina insisted that Mrs. Bardok drink from Marina's glass instead. Mrs. Bardok died four to five minutes after consuming the liquid.
